subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> are real number such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>p</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>q</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>369</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>p</mi></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>q</mi></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> are real number such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>p</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>q</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>369</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>p</mi></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>q</mi></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

p4+q4=369⇒p2+q22-2pq2=369

⇒p+q2-2pq2-2pq2=369

⇒9-2pq2-2pq2=369

⇒pq2-18pq-144=0  ⇒pq=24(rejected),-6

Now, 1p+1q-2=pq2p+q2=pq29=4

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be the roots of the equation $x^2+2 \sqrt{2} x-1=0$. The quadratic equation, whose roots are $\alpha^4+\beta^4$ and $\frac{1}{10}\left(\alpha^6+\beta^6\right)$, is :
question: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be the roots of the equation $x^2+2 \sqrt{2} x-1=0$. The quadratic equation, whose roots are $\alpha^4+\beta^4$ and $\frac{1}{10}\left(\alpha^6+\beta^6\right)$, is :
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x^2+2 \sqrt{2} x-1=0 \\ & \alpha+\beta=-2 \sqrt{2} \\ & \alpha \beta=-1 \\ & \alpha^4+\beta^4=\left(\alpha^2+\beta^2\right)^2-2 \alpha^2 \beta^2 \\ & =\left((\alpha+\beta)^2-2 \alpha \beta\right)^2-2(\alpha \beta)^2 \\ & =(8+2)^2-2(-1)^2 \\ & =100-2=98 \\ & \alpha^6+\beta^6=\left(\alpha^3+\beta^3\right)^2-2 \alpha^3 \beta^3 \\ & =\left((\alpha+\beta)\left((\alpha+\beta)^2-3 \alpha \beta\right)^2-2(\alpha \beta)^3\right.\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =(-2 \sqrt{2}(8+3))^2+2 \\ & =(8)(121)+2=970 \\ & \frac{1}{10}\left(\alpha^6+\beta^6\right)=97 \\ & x^2-(98+97) x+(98)(97)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2-195 x+9506=0\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let one root of the quadratic equation in $x$: $(k^2 - 15k + 27)x^2 + 9(k-1)x + 18 = 0$ be twice the other. Then the length of the latus rectum of the parabola $y^2 = 6kx$ is equal to:
question: Let one root of the quadratic equation in $x$: $(k^2 - 15k + 27)x^2 + 9(k-1)x + 18 = 0$ be twice the other. Then the length of the latus rectum of the parabola $y^2 = 6kx$ is equal to:
solution:
Let the roots of the given quadratic equation be $\alpha$ and $2\alpha$.

Sum of the roots:
$\alpha + 2\alpha = \dfrac{-9(k-1)}{k^2 - 15k + 27}$
$3\alpha = \dfrac{-9(k-1)}{k^2 - 15k + 27} \Rightarrow \alpha = \dfrac{-3(k-1)}{k^2 - 15k + 27}$

Product of the roots:
$\alpha \cdot 2\alpha = \dfrac{18}{k^2 - 15k + 27}$
$2\alpha^2 = \dfrac{18}{k^2 - 15k + 27} \Rightarrow \alpha^2 = \dfrac{9}{k^2 - 15k + 27}$

Substituting the value of $\alpha$ from the sum into the product equation:
$\left( \dfrac{-3(k-1)}{k^2 - 15k + 27} \right)^2 = \dfrac{9}{k^2 - 15k + 27}$

$\dfrac{9(k-1)^2}{(k^2 - 15k + 27)^2} = \dfrac{9}{k^2 - 15k + 27}$

Since $k^2 - 15k + 27 \neq 0$, we can simplify to:
$(k-1)^2 = k^2 - 15k + 27$

$k^2 - 2k + 1 = k^2 - 15k + 27$

$13k = 26 \Rightarrow k = 2$

The equation of the parabola is $y^2 = 6kx$. Substituting $k = 2$, we get:
$y^2 = 12x$

The length of the latus rectum of a parabola $y^2 = 4ax$ is $4a$, which is the coefficient of $x$.

Therefore, the length of the latus rectum is $12$.

Answer: $12$

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>2023</mn><msup><mi>α</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2024</mn><msup><mi>β</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msup></math>, then
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>2023</mn><msup><mi>α</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2024</mn><msup><mi>β</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></msup></math>, then
solution:
Given: α, β are roots of x2-x-1=0.

⇒α2-α-1=0

⇒α2=α+1   ...i

Also, Sn=2023αn+2024βn

⇒Sn-1+Sn-2=2023αn-1+2024βn-1+2023αn-2+2024βn-2

⇒Sn-1+Sn-2=2023αn-21+α+2024βn-21+β

Using equation i,

⇒Sn-1+Sn-2=2023αn-2α2+2024βn-2β2

⇒Sn-1+Sn-2=2023αn+2024βn

⇒Sn-1+Sn-2=Sn

Putting, n=12

⇒S12=S11+S10

q: If the equations <math> <mrow> <msup> <mi>x</mi> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <mn>2</mn> <mi>x</mi> <mo>+</mo> <mn>3</mn> <mo>=</mo> <mn>0</mn> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mi>a</mi><msup> <mi>x</mi> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> <math> <mrow> <mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo> </mrow> </math> have a common root, then <math> <mrow> <mi>a</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>c</mi> </mrow> </math> is:
question: If the equations <math> <mrow> <msup> <mi>x</mi> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <mn>2</mn> <mi>x</mi> <mo>+</mo> <mn>3</mn> <mo>=</mo> <mn>0</mn> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mi>a</mi><msup> <mi>x</mi> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> <math> <mrow> <mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo> </mrow> </math> have a common root, then <math> <mrow> <mi>a</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>c</mi> </mrow> </math> is:
solution:
As the roots of x 2 + 2 x + 3 = 0  are imaginary, hence both the roots are common with a x 2 +bx+c=0 .

∴a1=b2=c3.

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of points, where the curve f(x)=e8x-e6x-3e4x-e2x+1,x∈ℝ cuts x-axis, is equal to............
question: The number of points, where the curve f(x)=e8x-e6x-3e4x-e2x+1,x∈ℝ cuts x-axis, is equal to............
solution:
Given,

e8x-e6x-3e4x-e2x+1=0

⇒e4x-e2x-3-1e2x+1e4x=0

⇒e4x+1e4x+2-e2x+1e2x=5

⇒e2x+1e2x2-e2x+1e2x=3

Now let e2x+1e2x=t

So, the equation becomes

t2-t-5=0

⇒t=1+212, ignoring negative sign as exponential function are positive,

Now e2x+1e2x=1+212

⇒e4x-1+212e2x+1=0 which is quadratic equation in e2x with upward parabola, 

Now by A.M≥G.M we get, e2x+1e2x≥2

So, y=1+212>2, hence it will cut at two distinct point,

Hence, there will be two solution.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>21</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>21</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>17</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>17</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>19</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>19</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>21</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>21</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>17</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>17</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>19</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>19</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
solution:
Since, a and b are roots of x2-7x-1=0, so

a2-7a-1=0

⇒a2-1=7a

⇒a4+1-2a2=49a2

⇒a4+1=51a2   ...1

Similarly,

b4+1=51b2   ...2

Now,

a21+b21+a17+b17a19+b19

=a17a4+1+b17b4+1a19+b19

=51a19+51b19a19+b19

=51

q: If  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>α</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>β</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to:
question: If  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>α</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>β</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to:
solution:
Given α and β are the roots of the equation,7x2-3x-2=0, then 

α+β=37 ; αβ=-27

Now 

α1-α2+β1-β2=(α+β)-αβ(α+β)1-α21-β2=(α+β)-αβ(α+β)1+(αβ)2-α2+β2

⇒(α+β)-αβ(α+β)1+(αβ)2-(α+β)2+2αβ=37+27371+272-372-227=2716

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> are such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>i</mi></math> (here <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>i</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math>) is a root of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>α</mi><mi>z</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> are such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>i</mi></math> (here <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>i</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math>) is a root of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>α</mi><mi>z</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
solution:
∵α,β∈R⇒ other root is 1+2i

α=-( sum of roots )=-1-2i+1+2i=-2

β=product of roots =1-2i1+2i=5

∴α-β=-7

option 1

If <math><msub><mrow><mi>p</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>k</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>k</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>k</mi></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>k</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then which one of the following statements is not true?

=51+6

q: Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the roots of $x^2+\sqrt{3 x}-16=0$, and $\gamma$ and $\delta$ be the roots of $x^2+3 x-1=0$. If $P_n=\alpha^n+\beta^n$ and $Q_n=\gamma^n+\delta^n$, then $\frac{\mathrm{P}_{25}+\sqrt{3 \mathrm{P}_{24}}}{2 \mathrm{P}_{23}}+\frac{\mathrm{Q}_{25}-\mathrm{Q}_{23}}{\mathrm{Q}_{24}}$ is equal to
question: Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the roots of $x^2+\sqrt{3 x}-16=0$, and $\gamma$ and $\delta$ be the roots of $x^2+3 x-1=0$. If $P_n=\alpha^n+\beta^n$ and $Q_n=\gamma^n+\delta^n$, then $\frac{\mathrm{P}_{25}+\sqrt{3 \mathrm{P}_{24}}}{2 \mathrm{P}_{23}}+\frac{\mathrm{Q}_{25}-\mathrm{Q}_{23}}{\mathrm{Q}_{24}}$ is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x^2+\sqrt{3} \mathrm{x}-16=0 \lt \beta \\ & \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{n}}+\sqrt{3} \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{n}-1}-16 \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{n}-2}=0 \\ & \mathrm{P}_{25}+\sqrt{3} \mathrm{P}_{24}-16 \mathrm{P}_{23}=0 \\ & \therefore \frac{\mathrm{P}_{25}+\sqrt{3} \mathrm{P}_{24}}{2 \mathrm{P}_{23}}=8\end{aligned}$
Similarly
$x^2+3 x-1=0 \lt \sum_\delta^\gamma \quad Q_n=\gamma^n+\delta^n$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Q}_{25}-\mathrm{Q}_{23}=\gamma^{25}+\delta^{25}-\gamma^{23}-\delta^{23} \\ &=\gamma^{23}\left(\gamma^2-1\right)+\delta^{23}\left(\delta^2-1\right) \\ &=\gamma^{23}(-3 \gamma)+\delta^{23}(-3 \gamma) \\ &=-3\left[\gamma^{24}+\delta^{24}\right] \\ &=-3 \mathrm{Q}_{24} \\ & \therefore \frac{\mathrm{Q}_{25}-\mathrm{Q}_{23}}{\mathrm{Q}_{24}}=-3
\end{aligned}$
$\frac{\mathrm{P}_{25}+\sqrt{3} \mathrm{P}_{24}}{2 \mathrm{P}_{23}}+\frac{\mathrm{Q}_{25}-\mathrm{Q}_{23}}{\mathrm{Q}_{24}}=8-3=5$

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of the solutions of the equation <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi> </mi></math> is equal to
question: The sum of the solutions of the equation <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi> </mi></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

     x-2+xx-4+2=0
⇒x-2+x-4x+2=0 
⇒x-2+x2-4x+4=2       (Adding 2 on both sides)
⇒x-22+x-2=2
⇒t2+t-2=0 ; x-2=t ; t>0
⇒t=1or t=-2 ; Rejected because t>0
⇒x-2=1
⇒x=3 or x=1
⇒x=9 or x=1
Hence, the required sum =10.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> be the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></math> be the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. Then the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> are :
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> be the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></math> be the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. Then the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> are :
options: real and exactly one of them is positive
solution:
Given α,β be the roots of the equation x2-2x+6=0 

So sum of roots will be α+β=2 and product of roots will be αβ=6

And also given 1α2+1 and 1β2+1 are roots of x2+ax+b=0

So sum of roots will be -a=1α2+1+1β2+1

⇒a=-1α2-1β2-2 .....1

And similarly product of roots will be,

b=1α2+1β2+1+1α2β2 ....2

Now adding equation 1 & 2 we get,

a+b=1αβ2-1=16-1=-56 {as αβ=6}

Now putting the value of a+b in x2-a+b-2x+a+b+2=0

⇒x2--56-2x+2-56=0

⇒6x2+17x+7=0

⇒x=-73,x=-12 are the roots, both roots are real and negative.

question: Difference between the corresponding roots of $x^2+a x+b=0$ and $x^2+b x+a=0$ is same and $a \neq b$, then

subject: Mathematics
q: The product of all the rational roots of the equation $\left(x^2-9 x+11\right)^2-(x-4)(x-5)=3$, is equal to
question: The product of all the rational roots of the equation $\left(x^2-9 x+11\right)^2-(x-4)(x-5)=3$, is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \left(x^2-9 x+11\right)^2-(x-4)(x-5)=3 \\
& \left(x^2-9 x+11\right)^2-\left(x^2-9 x+20\right)=3
\end{aligned}$
Let $x^2-9 x+11=t$
$\begin{aligned}
& t^2-(t+9)=3 \\
& \Rightarrow t^2-t-12=0 \\
& \Rightarrow t^2-4 t+3 t-12=0 \\
& \Rightarrow t(t-4)+3(t-4)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow t=4 \text { or }-3 \\
& x^2-9 x+11=4 \\
& x^2-9 x+7=0
\end{aligned}$
Here, we will get irrational roots
$\begin{aligned}
& x^2-9 x+11=-3 \\
& x^2-9 x+14=0 \\
& x^2-7 x-2 x+14=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x=7,2 \\
& \Rightarrow \text { Product of all rational roots }=14
\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If the roots of the quadratic equation $x^2+p x+q=0$ are $\tan 30^{\circ}$ and $\tan 15^{\circ}$, respectively then the value of $2+q-p$ is
question: If the roots of the quadratic equation $x^2+p x+q=0$ are $\tan 30^{\circ}$ and $\tan 15^{\circ}$, respectively then the value of $2+q-p$ is
solution:
$x^2+p x+q=0$
$\tan 30^{\circ}+\tan 15^{\circ}=-p$
$\tan 30^{\circ} \cdot \tan 15^{\circ}=q$
$\tan 45^{\circ}=\frac{\tan 30^{\circ}+\tan 15^{\circ}}{1-\tan 30^{\circ} \tan 15^{\circ}}=\frac{-p}{1-q}=1$
$\Rightarrow-p=1-q$
$\Rightarrow q-p=1 \quad \therefore 2+q-p=3$.

subject: Mathematics
q: The integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>,</mo></math> for which the inequality <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>k</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>k</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math> is valid for every <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is:
question: The integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi><mo>,</mo></math> for which the inequality <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>k</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>k</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math> is valid for every <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is:
solution:
x2-23k-1x+8k2-7>0

Now, D<0

⇒ 43k-12-4×1×8k2-7<0

⇒ 9k2-6k+1-8k2+7<0

⇒ k2-6k+8<0

⇒ k-4k-2<0

⇒ k∈2,4

⇒k=3

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be the roots of the equation $x^2 - x + p = 0$ and $\gamma, \delta$ be the roots the equation $x^2 - 4x + q = 0$; $p, q \in \mathbf{Z}$. If $\alpha, \beta, \gamma, \delta$ are in G.P., then $|p + q|$ equals :
question: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be the roots of the equation $x^2 - x + p = 0$ and $\gamma, \delta$ be the roots the equation $x^2 - 4x + q = 0$; $p, q \in \mathbf{Z}$. If $\alpha, \beta, \gamma, \delta$ are in G.P., then $|p + q|$ equals :
solution:
Let $\alpha = a, \beta = ar, \gamma = ar^2, \delta = ar^3$ be the terms of the G.P.

From the given quadratic equations, the sum of the roots are:

$\alpha + \beta = 1 \Rightarrow a(1 + r) = 1$

$\gamma + \delta = 4 \Rightarrow ar^2(1 + r) = 4$

Dividing the second equation by the first equation:

$\dfrac{ar^2(1 + r)}{a(1 + r)} = \dfrac{4}{1}$

$r^2 = 4 \Rightarrow r = 2$ or $r = -2$

If $r = 2$, then $a(1 + 2) = 1 \Rightarrow a = \dfrac{1}{3}$.

The product of the roots of the first equation is $p = \alpha \beta = a^2 r = \left(\dfrac{1}{3}\right)^2 \times 2 = \dfrac{2}{9}$. Since $p \in \mathbf{Z}$, $r = 2$ is rejected.

If $r = -2$, then $a(1 - 2) = 1 \Rightarrow a = -1$.

The product of the roots of the first equation is $p = \alpha \beta = a^2 r = (-1)^2 \times (-2) = -2$.

The product of the roots of the second equation is $q = \gamma \delta = (ar^2)(ar^3) = a^2 r^5 = (-1)^2 \times (-2)^5 = -32$.

Both $p$ and $q$ are integers, which satisfies the given condition.

Therefore, $|p + q| = |-2 - 32| = |-34| = 34$.

Answer: $34$

subject: Mathematics
q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>∞</mo></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
question: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>∞</mo></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
solution:
Let x=3+14+13+14+13+…∞

So, x=3+14+1x=3+14x+1x

⇒x-3=x(4x+1)

⇒(4x+1)(x-3)=x

⇒4x2-12x+x-3=x

⇒4x2-12x-3=0

x=12±(12)2+12×42×4=12±12(16)8

=12±4×238=3±232

x=32±3=1.5±3.

But only positive value is accepted

So, x=1.5+3

subject: Mathematics
q: The equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>13</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> has :
question: The equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>13</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math> has :
options: four solutions two of which are negative
two solutions and both are negative
two solutions and only one of them is negative
solution:
Given,

e4x+8e3x+13e2x-8ex+1=0, x∈R

Now let ex=t we get,

t4+8t3+13t2-8t+1=0

Now divide complete equation by t2

⇒t2+1t2+8t-1t+13=0

⇒t-1t2+8t-1t+15=0

Now let t-1t=z we get,

⇒z2+8z+15=0

⇒z=-3,-5

So, t-1t=-3  or   t-1t=-5

⇒t2+3t-1=0  or   t2+5t-1=0

⇒t=-3±132,-5±292

So, ex=-3+132,-5+292=α,β (rejecting negative values as exponential is positive function)

And both -3+132 and -5+292∈0,1

So, x=lnα, lnβ are both negative,

Hence, there are two solution and both are negative.

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">β</mi></math> is equal to :
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">β</mi></math> is equal to :
solution:
Given equation is

2x2x+1=1⇒4x2+2x-1=0 .........(1)

roots of equation 1

are α and β

∴α+β=-12

⇒β=-12-α   ........(2)

and

4α2+2α-1=0⇒α2=14-α2 .........(3)

now

-2αα+1=-2α2-2a

=-214-α2-2α=-12-α=β

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>γ</mi></math> are three consecutive terms of a non-constant G.P. Such that the equations <math><mi>α</mi><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> have a common root, then <math><mi>α</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
question: If <math><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>γ</mi></math> are three consecutive terms of a non-constant G.P. Such that the equations <math><mi>α</mi><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> have a common root, then <math><mi>α</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
solution:
Given α, β,γ are in G.P.⇒β2=αγ
For the equation αx2+2βx+γ=0
D=4β2-4αγ=0
Hence roots of the equation are equal and equals to –βα=-γβ
Since given equation have common roots, hence -γβ must be a root of x2+x-1=0
⇒ γ2β2-γβ-1=0
⇒ γ2-γ β-β2=0
⇒ γ2=ββ+γ
⇒ γ.β2α=ββ+γ
⇒ αβ+γ=βγ

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of real solutions of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is:
question: The number of real solutions of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is:
solution:
We have, x2-x-12=0

⇒x2-4x+3x-12=0

⇒x+3x-4=0

⇒x=4 as x≠-3

⇒x=±2

Hence, number of real solution is 2.

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of integral values of <math><mi>m</mi></math> for which the equation, <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>m</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>m</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> has no real root, is
question: The number of integral values of <math><mi>m</mi></math> for which the equation, <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>m</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>m</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> has no real root, is
solution:
Since the given equation has no real solution.
⇒D<0
⇒41+3m2-4×1+m21+8m<0
⇒1+9m2+6m-1+8m+m2+8m3<0
⇒-2m2m-12<0
Clearly, for infinite values of m given condition is true.

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>3</mn><msqrt><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mn>5</mn></msqrt></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mn>5</mn><msqrt><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mn>3</mn></msqrt></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mn>3</mn><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mn>5</mn><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> then the equation, whose roots are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>β</mi></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>α</mi></mfrac></math>,
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>3</mn><msqrt><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mn>5</mn></msqrt></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mn>5</mn><msqrt><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mn>3</mn></msqrt></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mn>3</mn><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mn>5</mn><msup><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> then the equation, whose roots are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>β</mi></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>α</mi></mfrac></math>,
solution:
Given α,β are the roots of the equation x2-5+3log35-5log53x+33log3513-5log5323-1=0

Now 3log35-5log53=3log35-3log35log53

=3log35-3log35=0

Also 3log3513-5log5323=5log5323-5log5323

=0

So, given equation becomes x2-5x-3=0 

i.e. α+β=5; αβ=-3

Now if the roots are α+1β and β+1α

i.e., αβ+1β&αβ+1α 

i.e., -2β & -2α

Then let -2α=t⇒α=-2t

As α2-5α-3=0

⇒-2t2-5-2t-3=0

⇒4t2+10t-3=0

⇒3t2-10t-4=0

i.e., 3x2-10x-4=0 is the required equation.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let λ≠0 be in R. If α and β are the roots of the equation x2-x+2λ=0, and α and γ are the roots of the equation 3x2-10x+27λ=0, then βγλ is equal to ________.
question: Let λ≠0 be in R. If α and β are the roots of the equation x2-x+2λ=0, and α and γ are the roots of the equation 3x2-10x+27λ=0, then βγλ is equal to ________.
solution:
α+β=1   ...i

αβ=2λ   ...ii
α+γ=103   ...iii
αγ=9λ   ...iv
from iv & ii
γβ=92   ...v

from iii-i

γ-β=103-1=73   ...vi

from v and vi

β=23,γ=3
from i α=1-β=1-23=13
from ii λ=αβ2=13×232=19
So βγλ=23×319=18

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> be a quadratic polynomial such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>–</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If one of the roots of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>, then its other root lies in
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> be a quadratic polynomial such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>–</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If one of the roots of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>, then its other root lies in
solution:
Let fx=ax2+bx+c be a quadratic equation with roots α, β

f2+f-1=0⇒5a+b+2c=0

 Also f3=0⇒9a+3b+c=0

⇒a-5=b13=c6

Product of roots αβ=ca=-65

We know α=3⇒β=-25∈-1,0

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mfenced><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>></mo><mi>β</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mi>n</mi></msup></math> for each positive integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>17</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>20</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>17</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>19</mn></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>18</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>19</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msubsup><mi>P</mi><mn>18</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mfenced><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>></mo><mi>β</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mi>n</mi></msup></math> for each positive integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>17</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>20</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>17</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>19</mn></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>18</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>19</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msubsup><mi>P</mi><mn>18</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given, x2+52x+10=0

and  Pn=αn-βn 

Now P17P20+52P17P19P18P19+52P182=P17P20+52P19P18P19+52P18

=P17α20-β20+52α19-β19P18α19-β19+52α18-β18

=P17α19α+52-β19β+52P18α18α+52-β18β+52

Since α+52=-10/α       ...1

& β+52=-10/β       ...2

Now put these values in above expression

P17α19α+52-β19β+52P18α18α+52-β18β+52=-10P17P18-10P18P17=1

subject: Mathematics
q: The value of $\alpha$ for which the sum of the squares of the roots of the equation $x^2-(a-2) x-a-1=0$ assume the least value is
question: The value of $\alpha$ for which the sum of the squares of the roots of the equation $x^2-(a-2) x-a-1=0$ assume the least value is
solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x^2-(a-2) x-a-1=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \alpha+\beta=a-2 \\
& \alpha \beta=-(a+1) \\
& \alpha^2+\beta^2=(\alpha+\beta)^2-2 \alpha \beta \\
& \quad=a^2-2 a+6=(a-1)^2+5 \\
& \Rightarrow a=1 .
\end{aligned}
$$
